title: Human Value Alignment and AI Decision-Making

visuals.title: Human Value Alignment and AI Decision-Making
visuals.description: An animated diagram showing the core human values (Fairness, Transparency, Accountability), the Value Alignment Framework steps, key strategies, and real-world case studies for ethical AI design.
narrationSequence: Human Value Alignment ensures AI systems act in accordance with human values like fairness, transparency, and accountability.
At the core of value alignment are three pillars: Fairness, Transparency, and Accountability. These guide every design decision.
The Value Alignment Framework follows three steps: Identify values, Design AI to reflect them, then ensure Explainability in decisions.
Key strategies include Value Prioritization to rank what matters most, Value-Based Design to embed those priorities, and rigorous Testing and Evaluation.
Real-world examples: A company uses value-based promotion decisions ensuring fairness, and a healthcare AI provides explainable diagnoses for trust.
Key takeaway: Value alignment requires a systematic framework of identifying values, embedding them in design, and continuous testing to ensure AI promotes social good.

diagrams.title: Value Alignment Process
elements: Design AI Systems to Reflect Values
connections: Identify Human Values -> Prioritize Values (Fairness, Transparency, Accountability)
Prioritize Values (Fairness, Transparency, Accountability) -> Design AI Systems to Reflect Values
Design AI Systems to Reflect Values -> Ensure Explainability and Transparency
Ensure Explainability and Transparency -> Test and Evaluate for Alignment
Test and Evaluate for Alignment -> Deploy Ethical AI
